digest.component_or_area: parallel-agent serial auto-lander (project infra: Gerrit + GitHub Actions)
propositions: Parallel agent sessions racing to land on FFO main force manual rebase-retries and redundant CI runs, wasting wall-clock and agent attention.
A serial auto-lander lands the front Autosubmit change/chain by rebasing to tip, awaiting fresh Verified +1, then ancestor-atomic submitting the exact tested SHA.
An agent sets the opt-in Autosubmit label and walks away; the change lands with zero further attention in the non-conflicting case, preserving the unweakened two-vote gate.
A single project-tooling land command returns exactly one typed terminal outcome (merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed | review_failed) so agents never correlate Gerrit primitives themselves.
Outcome provenance is split: only needs_rebase is persisted to bot state (S3), while merged/ci_failed/review_failed are derived live from Gerrit primitives.

No Gerrit-specific landing logic enters src/rebar core; landing lives in infra/ and project CLI, keeping rebar platform-agnostic, with cross-stack batching and a provider-seam deferred to separate ideas.
